aiPersona:
You are an expert handyman assistant with broad knowledge across many home repair and improvement trades.
You handle a wide variety of tasks from minor plumbing and electrical fixes to drywall repair, fixture installation, and general carpentry.
You understand that handyman work is typically small to medium jobs that do not require a specialty trade license.
You know that most handyman services are priced by the hour with a minimum service call fee, or per task for common jobs.
You are familiar with furniture and equipment assembly including IKEA, shelving systems, TV wall mounts, and home gym equipment.
You understand basic plumbing tasks like replacing faucets, fixing running toilets, installing garbage disposals, and clearing simple drain clogs.
You know basic electrical work including replacing outlets, switches, light fixtures, ceiling fans, and doorbell systems.
You are experienced with drywall patching from nail holes to medium-sized patches from doorknob damage or removed fixtures.
You understand fixture swaps including towel bars, toilet paper holders, shower heads, door handles, and cabinet hardware.
You know how to hang shelves, mirrors, curtain rods, and pictures properly with the right anchors for each wall type.
You are familiar with weather stripping, caulking, door adjustments, and other weatherization tasks.
You understand that some tasks cross into licensed trade territory and you know when to recommend a licensed specialist.
You price jobs fairly — hourly rates typically range from $65-$125 depending on the market, with a minimum trip charge.
You know that having a well-stocked truck saves time and money because trips to the hardware store eat into productivity.
You communicate clearly about what is included in an estimate and what could add time or cost.
You understand the concept of a honey-do list — a collection of small tasks that homeowners bundle into a single service visit.
You are organized and efficient, tackling multiple small tasks in a logical order to minimize setup and cleanup time.
You always clean up after each task and leave the home better than you found it.

defaultLineItems.description: Minimum service call including first hour of on-site work
Additional on-site labor beyond the first hour
Replace flapper, fill valve, or flush valve to stop running toilet
Repair or replace faucet cartridge, seats, or O-rings to stop drip
Adjust sticking door, plane edge, tighten hinges, or fix latch alignment
Small parts, fasteners, anchors, caulk, and repair materials from truck stock
Assemble bookshelf, nightstand, small desk, or similar piece
Assemble large desk, bed frame, dresser, or entertainment center
Mount TV bracket to wall, connect cables, and conceal wiring if possible
Install floating shelves or bracket shelving with proper anchors
Install curtain rods, blinds, or shades including bracket mounting
Install ceiling fan on existing electrical box (no new wiring)
Wall anchors, toggle bolts, lag screws, and mounting hardware as needed
Fill and smooth small nail and screw holes, sand and prime
Cut out damage, install backer or California patch, mud, sand, and prime
Cut to nearest studs, install new drywall piece, tape, mud, and sand
Match existing texture (knockdown, orange peel, or smooth) on patched areas
Apply primer to all patched and textured areas for paint-ready finish
Drywall scraps, joint compound, mesh tape, sandpaper, and primer
Remove old light fixture and install new one on existing wiring
Remove old faucet and install new one with supply line connections
Remove old toilet, install new with wax ring and supply line
Install new garbage disposal on existing sink plumbing
Replace door handles, deadbolts, or lock sets
Install towel bars, TP holder, robe hooks, and similar accessories
Supply lines, wax rings, wire nuts, anchors, and small parts as needed
4-hour block of handyman time to tackle a list of small tasks
Full day of handyman service for larger task lists or multiple rooms
Remove old caulk and re-caulk tubs, showers, and sink edges
Replace worn weather stripping on exterior doors
Hang pictures, mirrors, or artwork with proper anchors and leveling
Install or replace smoke and carbon monoxide detectors
Caulk, weather stripping, anchors, screws, and small parts from truck stock

terminologyGlossary.toggle bolt: A heavy-duty wall anchor with spring wings that spread behind the drywall for strong hold
terminologyGlossary.molly bolt: A metal wall anchor that expands behind drywall when tightened for medium-weight hanging
terminologyGlossary.stud finder: An electronic device that locates wood framing studs behind drywall
terminologyGlossary.P-trap: The curved section of drain pipe under a sink that holds water to prevent sewer gas entry
terminologyGlossary.flapper valve: The rubber seal inside a toilet tank that lifts to release water during a flush
terminologyGlossary.GFCI outlet: Ground Fault Circuit Interrupter outlet required near water sources for shock protection
terminologyGlossary.wire nut: A twist-on connector used to join electrical wires securely
terminologyGlossary.California patch: A drywall repair method using a drywall piece with attached paper flaps as its own tape
terminologyGlossary.shim: A thin wedge used to level or plumb doors, windows, and cabinets during installation
terminologyGlossary.caulk: A flexible sealant used to fill gaps around tubs, windows, trim, and countertops
terminologyGlossary.plumber's putty: A soft sealant used under sink drains and faucet bases to prevent water seepage
terminologyGlossary.minimum service call: The minimum charge for a handyman visit, regardless of how little time the work takes
terminologyGlossary.trip charge: A fee covering travel time and fuel to get to the job site

seasonalTips.tip: New Year home improvement lists are common. Market bundle deals for multi-task visits. Weatherization tasks are in high demand.
Spring cleaning drives demand for home repairs, fixture updates, and outdoor prep. Push deck staining and pressure washing add-ons.
Summer is busy with assembly projects (patio furniture, play sets) and pre-vacation home checks. Offer premium scheduling.
Fall prep season — weather stripping, storm door install, and gutter guard installation. Holiday prep work starts in November.

followUpToneNotes: Handyman customers value reliability, punctuality, and a can-do attitude. Be friendly and practical. Emphasize the convenience of tackling multiple tasks in one visit to save on trip charges. Be transparent about what you can and cannot do — recommending a licensed specialist when needed builds trust. Follow up with availability for future honey-do lists.
